text.title: W3C Design Tokens Format Rules
text.summary: Field rules for the W3C Design Tokens Community Group draft format, including required fields, supported types, and alias graph requirements.

rule.statement: Every token must have a `$type` and `$value` field; add `$description` to every semantic token to record design intent.
rule.rationale: `$description` is the only place intent is recorded; without it, future maintainers (and LLMs) can only guess why a token exists.

rule.statement: Use `$ref` aliases for primitive-to-semantic-to-component layering; never duplicate values across tokens.
rule.rationale: Value duplication defeats the migration purpose: when the primitive changes, aliases update automatically; duplicates go stale silently.

example.note: Minimal W3C-compliant token file with color and alias

list.item: color — hex, rgb(), oklch(); always include color-space metadata for wide-gamut values.
dimension — with unit (px, rem, dp); never unitless.
fontFamily — string or array of strings.
shadow — composite type; sub-schema has evolved across draft versions, validate each composite.
typography — composite; evolving sub-schema, lint against current draft.

rule.statement: Pin a draft snapshot date in the repo and validate against that snapshot — do not auto-pull the latest spec.
rule.rationale: The format is a Community Group draft with possible breaking changes; pinning prevents silent spec-upgrade breakage in CI.

rule.statement: Preserve the full alias graph when migrating; never flatten aliases by embedding primitive values in semantic tokens.
rule.rationale: Flattening defeats the migration purpose: primitive changes stop propagating to semantic and component layers.

antipattern.description: Migrating tokens by replacing `$ref` aliases with resolved hex values.
antipattern.reason: The alias graph is the primary value of the W3C format; a flattened file behaves identically to the legacy format being replaced.

text.reference: Minimal W3C-compliant example file with color, dimension, and alias tokens.
CI validation script against the W3C draft JSON Schema.
